2604.21751.title: Why are all LLMs Obsessed with Japanese Culture? On the Hidden Cultural and Regional Biases of LLMs
2604.21751.paper_summary: LLMs have been showing limitations when it comes to cultural coverage and competence, and in some cases show regional biases such as amplifying Western and Anglocentric viewpoints. While there have been works analysing the cultural capabilities of LLMs, there has not been specific work on highlighting LLM regional preferences when it comes to cultural-related questions. In this work, we propose a new dataset based on a comprehensive taxonomy of Culture-Related Open Questions (CROQ). The results show that, contrary to previous cultural bias work, LLMs show a clear tendency towards countries such as Japan. Moveover, our results show that when prompting in languages such as English or other high-resource ones, LLMs tend to provide more diverse outputs and show less inclinations towards answering questions highlighting countries for which the input language is an official language. Finally, we also investigate at which point of LLM training this cultural bias emerges, with our results suggesting that the first clear signs appear after supervised fine-tuning, and not during pre-training.
2604.21751.paper_summary_zh: LLMs 在文化覆蓋和能力方面顯示出局限性，在某些情況下顯示出區域性偏見，例如放大西方和英語中心的觀點。雖然已有研究分析 LLMs 的文化能力，但尚未有專門的研究突顯 LLM 在文化相關問題上的區域偏好。在這項工作中，我們提出了一個基於文化相關開放問題 (CROQ) 的全面分類法的新數據集。結果顯示，與先前的文化偏見研究相反，LLMs 明顯傾向於像日本這樣的國家。此外，我們的結果顯示，當使用英語或其他高資源語言進行提示時，LLMs 傾向於提供更多樣化的輸出，並對於回答強調輸入語言為官方語言的國家的問題顯示出較少的傾向。最後，我們還調查了 LLM 訓練的哪個階段出現這種文化偏見，我們的結果表明，第一個明顯的跡象出現在監督微調之後，而不是在預訓練期間。
2604.21751.author: Joseba Fernandez de Landa et.al.
2604.21751.authors: Joseba Fernandez de Landa, Carla Perez-Almendros, Jose Camacho-Collados
